tour.createHint: Click + to create your first document
tour.slashHint: Type / anywhere in the editor to open the menu
tour.mathInlineHint: Try typing $$x$$ to create inline math
tour.mathBlockHint: Try typing $$$x$$$ to create standalone display math

sidebar.title: Collapse the sidebar
sidebar.body: Use this toggle to collapse or expand the sidebar and free up space.

newDoc.title: Create a document
newDoc.body: Click the + next to Documents to create your first document.

toolbar.title: Formatting toolbar
toolbar.body: The toolbar covers almost every formatting feature — grouped by section:

slash.title: Slash commands
slash.body: In the editor, type / to insert headings, lists, tables, images, math and more.

mathInline.title: Inline math shortcut
mathInline.body: Type $$ followed by a formula and $$ again, right after existing text, to insert inline math without opening a dialog.

mathBlock.title: Display math shortcut
mathBlock.body: On an empty line, type $$$ followed by a formula and $$$ again to insert a standalone display formula.

outline.title: Outline
outline.body: Lists every heading in the document — click one to jump straight to that section, handy for navigating long documents.

metadata.title: Document Info
metadata.body: Fill in title, author, date, and other metadata. Fields adjust automatically based on your chosen template, and some can be left blank.

template.title: Template
template.body: Switch the document's layout template. Each one has its own fonts and heading style, plus its own customization options.

export.title: Export
export.body: Export the document as PDF, DOCX, or Markdown — the PDF export also lets you toggle whether a table of contents is included.

history.title: Version History
history.body: The document snapshots automatically at an interval you set. Preview any snapshot, and restoring never loses your current content — it's snapshotted first.

footnote.title: Footnotes
footnote.body: Besides the toolbar button, you can insert a footnote right after any text. Its content always shows at the bottom of the document, separate from the body — good for citations or asides without breaking up the reading flow.

crossRef.title: Cross-references
crossRef.body: Type @ to reference any figure, table, equation, or heading in the document. Figures and tables number themselves automatically; equations only get a number once something actually references them. Clicking an inserted reference jumps straight to its target.

imageCrop.title: Image cropping
imageCrop.body: Click an image you've already inserted to crop it right there in place, no separate image editor needed. Not happy with the crop? Revert back to the original in one click, any time.

itemHints.footnote: Adds a footnote, pinned to the bottom

tour.crossRefHint: Type @ anywhere in the editor to open the menu

editor.writePlaceholder: Start typing, or press "/" to open the command menu
editor.emptyDescription: Select a document from the sidebar, or create a new one to start editing.
editor.oldDocReset: This note was created in the old editor and couldn’t be migrated automatically, so it’s now a blank page.

slash.imageRowHint: Insert two or more images in a row
slash.statsChartHint: Bar, stacked bar, pie, line, scatter, or box chart

image.unsupportedFormat: Unsupported image format — use PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP, SVG, or PDF
image.pdfRenderFailed: Failed to generate a PDF preview — check the file isn't encrypted or corrupted

math.inlineHint: Inline math flows within the line of text.
math.displayHint: Display math appears on its own line — good for longer formulas.
math.emptyPreview: Once you type LaTeX, the preview appears here.
math.invalid: Invalid equation — check the LaTeX syntax.
math.refNamePlaceholder: e.g. Formula name, used in the cross-reference list

functionPlot.thicknessOutOfRange: Line thickness must be between {min} and {max}
functionPlot.previewError: Couldn’t render — check the formula syntax.

statsChart.importError: Couldn't read that file — check it's a valid CSV/Excel/OpenDocument spreadsheet.
statsChart.tooManyEntriesWarning: Too many data points can make the chart feel crowded.
statsChart.label: Label

crossRef.brokenHint: The referenced target could not be found — it may have been deleted
crossRef.empty: No figures, tables, equations, or headings to reference yet

imageRow.tooFewImages: Please select at least two images
imageRow.captionPlaceholder: Add a caption for the whole group

documents.title: Documents
documents.subtitle: Your notes and handouts sync automatically.
documents.emptyHint: Create your first document to get started.
documents.deleteDescription: “{title}” will be permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.

projects.title: Projects
projects.emptyHint: Create your first project to start grouping documents.
projects.name: Name
projects.deleteDescription: “{name}” will be deleted. Its documents become unfiled.

templates.title: Templates
templates.subtitle: Choose a layout for your document. Switching templates won’t affect existing content.

plain-note.name: Plain Note
plain-note.description: A clean page with no metadata. Just write.

lecture-note.name: Lecture Note
lecture-note.description: Good for organizing course content; add the course, lecturer, and date.

academic-handout.name: Academic Handout
academic-handout.description: Good for formal handouts; add the author, institution, and abstract.

problem-set.name: Problem Set
problem-set.description: Good for problem sets; set a due date and toggle solutions.

reading-note.name: Reading Note
reading-note.description: Good for reading notes; record the author, pages, and a verdict.

bananote.name: Bananote
bananote.description: A more colorful note with a playful banana accent.

bubble.name: Bubble
bubble.description: Good for class presentations; set a title, subtitle, and accent color.

flow-way.name: Flow Way
flow-way.description: Good for general reports; add a subtitle and table of contents.

hetvid.name: Hetvid
hetvid.description: Good for academic papers; set the abstract, version, and language.

kunskap.name: Kunskap
kunskap.description: Good for course reports; add the author, course, and paper size.

typsidian.name: Typsidian
typsidian.description: Good for Obsidian-style notes; add a course name and switch light/dark colors.

obelisk.name: Obelisk
obelisk.description: Good for minimalist notes with sidenotes and theorem boxes; a clean baseline-grid layout.

metropole-report.name: Metropole Report
metropole-report.description: Good for reports with a cover page; set a subtitle, accent color, and paper size.

elsearticle.name: Elsearticle
elsearticle.description: Good for journal submissions; add an abstract, journal name, and column count.

gakusyun-doc.name: Gakusyun Doc
gakusyun-doc.description: Good for Chinese-language reports; add a subtitle and turn on a cover page or index.

jastylest-zh.name: Jastylest ZH
jastylest-zh.description: Good for Chinese/Japanese articles; add a course name and abstract.

minicise.name: Minicise
minicise.description: Good for exercise sheets; add a course, semester, and date to the header.

mousse-notes.name: Mousse Notes
mousse-notes.description: Good for book-style lecture notes with a title page and theorem environments.

folklore.name: Folklore
folklore.description: Good for fiction manuscripts; a half-letter book layout with a preface and chapters.

rubber-article.name: Rubber Article
rubber-article.description: Good for classic-style articles; add authors and turn on numbered headings.

toffee-tufte.name: Toffee Tufte
toffee-tufte.description: Tufte-style academic report with margin sidenotes instead of traditional footnotes.

placeholders.author: e.g. Anthony Sung
placeholders.source: e.g. Rudin, Principles of Mathematical Analysis

templates.noPreviewAvailable: No preview for this template yet

panel.outlineEmpty: Headings will appear here as you write.
panel.metadataEmpty: This template has no fields to fill in.
panel.metadataHint: These fields come from the template; optional ones can be left blank.
panel.historyEmptyOff: Version snapshots are currently off — turn them on in Settings.
panel.historyRestoreDescription: Your current content is snapshotted first, so you can always undo this if you change your mind.

export.title: Export
export.subtitle: Preview the export and output the current document as a PDF.
export.payloadHint: This is the data sent when you export.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: These settings are stored only on this device.

export.title: Export defaults
export.description: Set the defaults used when exporting a document.
export.folderBlocked: The browser can’t access this folder. Choose another location.
export.folderUnsupported: Your browser does not support folder picking.

backup.title: Backup
backup.description: Export your documents as Markdown for safekeeping.
backup.allDocumentsHint: Exports all {count} documents as a zip of Markdown files, organized by project.
backup.importHint: Restore from a .md file or a backup .zip.

update.title: Software update
update.description: Check whether a newer version of AnvilNote Desktop is available.

versionHistory.title: Version History
versionHistory.description: Automatically snapshot documents over time so you can restore an earlier version.
versionHistory.intervalHint: A snapshot is only created when content has actually changed, so it never wastes space.

documentDefaults.title: Document Defaults
documentDefaults.description: Starting values automatically filled into new documents. You can still change them per-document afterwards.
documentDefaults.author: Default author
documentDefaults.authorHint: Filled into a new document's Author field, for templates that have one.

hero.label: Local-first writing workspace
hero.kicker: AnvilNote keeps editing, math, code, templates, and export in one workspace so you can focus on structuring long-form writing.

valueStrip.1: Use blocks to structure long-form writing without fighting layout
valueStrip.2: Preview, apply templates, and export when the draft is ready

localFirst.title: Local-first by default
localFirst.body: No account is required, and everyday writing is not tied to a cloud service. Documents, drafts, and revisions stay on your machine.

structuredWriting.title: Built for structured writing
structuredWriting.body: It is more than a blank page. Headings, paragraphs, lists, math, code, and images can live in the same document while long-form writing keeps a clear structure.

exportWorkflow.title: One export workflow
exportWorkflow.body: Templates, previews, and export belong to the same workflow. When the note is ready, you do not need to move it into another tool just to finish formatting.

showcase.title: Templates that finish the document
showcase.description:
AnvilNote goes beyond the editor so your content can continue into handouts, reports, and polished notes.
Most templates come from open-source packages in the Typst ecosystem, integrated and ready to use.

closing.title: A quiet workspace for writing, organizing, and finishing.
closing.description: From draft to export, the whole workflow stays in one place. No sign-in and no bouncing between multiple tools.

demo.exportDisabled: PDF export is available in the full workspace

toast.exportMocked: No renderer is connected yet — this is a mock export.
toast.importSucceeded: Imported {count, plural, one {# document} other {# documents}}.

pdfPreview.pdfPreviewUnavailable: PDF preview is unavailable right now

about.description:
Forge your ideas into notes.
An offline-first writing app with built-in Typst typesetting and PDF export.

fontsTab.hint: All fonts bundled with AnvilNote and their licenses.

dependenciesTab.hint: Open-source packages used by AnvilNote and their licenses.
dependenciesTab.name: Package
